version: Decoded from firmware B.8.8 / GDT v6.6
status: FULLY DECODED — confirmed with working OLT SFP stick

transport.host_requirement: Must be on same L2 segment. Media converter works. MikroTik switches may filter 0x88B6 frames even with HW offload disabled.

ethernet_header.dst_request: ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff (broadcast for discovery) or OLT MAC (after discovery)

payload.total_size_bytes: Variable (min 50 for requests, larger for responses)

fields.name: magic
fields.description: Protocol identifier, constant in all packets

fields.name: sequence
fields.description: Auto-incrementing per request. Response echoes the request's sequence number.

fields.name: length
fields.description: Length of the data portion after the header. Requests typically 0x000C (12). Responses vary (13-268+).

fields.name: message_group
fields.description: Message category/group. For requests this is Get=0x00xx. For responses the high byte is 0x01 (GetResponse offset +0x0100).

fields.name: parameter_id
fields.description: The actual parameter being queried within the message group. This is the real message identifier.

fields.name: data
fields.description: Request: zeros for Get operations, values for Set operations. Response: parameter value data.

message_group_encoding.description: The message_group field (bytes 8-9) encodes both the operation type and a group category.

request_groups.0x0000: System group (OltVersion, OltUpTime, Temperature, Voltage, DNA, etc.)
request_groups.0x0002: ONU/Config group (OnuStatus, ONU operations, per-ONU queries)

parameter_map.description: The parameter_id (byte 10) combined with message_group determines what is being queried/set.

0x09.name: FpgaBitstream
0x09.response_format: Raw FPGA configuration header including sync word and IDCODE
0x09.note: This is a large response containing the FPGA bitstream header

onu_group_0x0002.description: Per-ONU queries. Target byte (offset 11) specifies ONU index 0x00-0x1F.

discovery_sequence.description: The VOLT tool performs continuous polling in a repeating cycle
initial_discovery: 1. Send Get group=0x0000 param=0x00 target=0xFF (OltVersion) to broadcast
2. OLT responds with firmware version string → tool now knows OLT MAC
3. Send Get group=0x0000 param=0x09 target=0xFF (FpgaBitstream) to OLT MAC
4. OLT responds with FPGA header including DNA and IDCODE
5. Tool displays OLT card with version, temperature, uptime
polling_cycle: Get OltUpTime (param=0x01, also via group 0x0001 param=0x18/0x19)
Iterate ONU slots 0-31: Get OnuStatus + OnuGponSn per slot
discovery_sequence.cycle_rate: Continuous, approximately 46 unique queries per cycle

observed_olt_data.note: This OLT SFP was previously thought defective (no response through MikroTik switches). Works perfectly through a dumb media converter.

key_findings.sequence_echo: Response echoes the request's sequence number exactly
key_findings.sub_field_is_real_param: The 'sub' byte (offset 10) is the actual parameter ID, not the msg_id field
key_findings.first_discovery_broadcast: First packet is broadcast, subsequent can be unicast to OLT MAC
key_findings.media_converter_required: MikroTik CRS354/CCR2004 do NOT pass 0x88B6 frames even with HW offload disabled. A dumb media converter (L1 PHY) works.
key_findings.variable_response_length: Response length field varies: 13 (simple values), 17 (strings), 20 (DNA), 268 (FPGA bitstream)

security.reset_method: Dealer CODE + OLT power cycle within 30 seconds

defective_captures: pcap/volt.pcapng (21,710 requests, 0 responses — defective OLT or switch filtering)
